head: 48329b7 2026-07-13 15:27:35 +0200
branches_note: Default branch is origin/dev (origin/HEAD -> dev). origin/dev is 34 commits AHEAD of origin/main (main-only=0) — the ENG-2069 nothing-on-main hazard: main lags the real work by 34 commits. origin/crew/daniel is byte-identical to origin/dev (0 divergence). Audited origin/dev as the authoritative ref. Fetch succeeded.

E1-S1.note: Scaffold fully present (module github.com/orvexai/orvex-cli, cmd/ + internal/ trees, a real CI workflow with gofmt/vet/build/test + private-module OIDC). The AGPL-import guard is only TestContentImportGraphNoAGPLAdjacent (guard_importgraph_test.go) — a package-scoped `go list -deps .` docmost-substring check on internal/content, whose OWN comment admits it is 'NOT wired into any CI'. The DoD's named test TestAGPLImportGuardRejectsDocmostPackages, a fixture-module-graph guard, and the AC4 transitive-dependency detection do not exist. ci.yml has no dedicated AGPL-guard step.

E1-S2.note: Full per-service endpoint registry: ServiceID set (wiki-api/knowledge/events/ai/identity), Resolve() with explicit per-service ORVEX_*_URL overrides winning over ORVEX_URL gateway-derived flat hosts, DOCMOST_* warn-once aliases (firstEnv). config_routing_test.go covers routing.

E1-S3.note: Single chokepoint Transport.Do present: correlation X-Request-Id minted once + echoed on errors, per-service envelope decode (decodeServiceError accepts both `code` and `error_code`), honest codeForStatus HTTP→vocabulary mapping. BUT retries on idempotent methods and Idempotency-Key minting on writes are explicitly TODO(scaffold C1) — the package doc (line 28-30) states they 'remain scaffold-stub'. The only retry path implemented is the 421 re-resolve.

E1-S4.note: Per-service typed clients (WikiClient/KnowledgeClient/AIClient/IdentityClient/IssueClient) exist but are HAND-AUTHORED shells (clients.go doc: 'these hand-authored shells mark the seam until the generator lands'); gen/ is a placeholder (doc.go only, no generated types). No codegen pipeline from the orvex-studio-contracts tag exists. The CI 'contract drift gate' (TestCliContractNoDrift) is the error-vocabulary/exit-code ordering golden — NOT a contract-codegen drift gate.

E1-S5.note: 421 Misdirected-Request re-resolve leg fully implemented: MisdirectedRequest/ReResolveDirective/DiscoveryResponse types hand-mirrored from contracts tag v0.1.3, Do() decodes the 421 envelope, reResolveCell() re-resolves via discovery endpoint (carrying the same bearer) and retransmits exactly once. Covered by transport_test.go. Residual: cold-starting an events-cursor bound to the old host is not done (no cursor exists in the client package — it lives in internal/daemon).

E2-S1.note: Namespace-first cobra tree (wiki/search/ai/auth/admin/migrate service namespaces + daemon/cache/config/doctor/audit/instructions/version client-local groups). Hidden back-compat aliases (page/space/comment/label/attach) registered in legacy.go, each emitting a typed deprecation pointer with WithNext.

E2-S2.note: Full output contract: NewRenderer auto-resolves json-when-piped/human-on-TTY (isTTY), --output/--fields/--id-only/--compact projection, SetEscapeHTML(false), stderr-for-progress/stdout-for-data, deterministic human formatter with sorted keys. contract_test + envelope_test cover it.

E2-S3.note: Frozen 0-9 ExitCode range + shared errorCode vocabulary (ErrorCodeRegistry in declaration order) + exitFor mapping. TestCliContractNoDrift (contract_test.go) parses errors.go via go/parser and byte-matches the const ordering + exitFor against committed goldens; wired as a named CI required check (ci.yml:71).

E2-S4.note: Two variants via build tags: fullfeat.Enabled + Require() (stub returns REQUIRES_FULL_BINARY honestly; full is a no-op). Makefile build/build-full; ci.yml compiles AND tests both the default and -tags full variants (with cgo-toolchain assertion for tree-sitter). Stub/full test pairs exist for screenshot.

E2-S5.note: docmost-cli behaviour-parity harness present: TestByteParityCorpus (PM fixtures lifted verbatim into testdata/corpus/content), TestWikiVerbParity + TestWikiContentCarryingVerbParityCompleteness (command-tree walk fails closed on an uncovered content-carrying verb). All wired as a named CI required check (ci.yml:86).

E3-S1.note: Full page CRUD + verb grammar: get/list/create/update/patch/upsert/delete against wiki-api v1 (POST /v1/wiki, GET /v1/wiki/{loc}, PUT/PATCH /v1/pages, /v1/pages/upsert, bulk delete). CAS ifVersion → typed VERSION_MISMATCH; UpsertPage surfaces DUPLICATE_CANDIDATE (exit 8); LOSSY_WRITE_FORMAT_REJECTED gate before any request. wiki_page_test.go covers them. QUOTA_EXCEEDED is surfaced via the shared decodeServiceError path (in vocab).

E3-S2.note: nav (tree/outline/breadcrumbs/backlinks/recent/resolve-slug/watchers/permissions/transclusion) and governance (supersede/status/verify/label) verbs + WikiClient methods implemented; wiki_verbs_test.go covers them. Caveat: some nav leaf backend routes are flagged known-broken/unverified server-side (wiki.go:270 note) — the CLI side is complete but end-to-end depends on wiki-api routes that may not all exist yet.

E3-S3.note: Diagram/embed authoring via wikiDiagramCmd: builds a PM opaque node, round-trips it through content.PMToDfM/DfMToPM to prove byte-parity, then CAS-updates. image_from_prompt is covered by `ai image` (E5-S2). But there is NO dedicated authoring surface for the '21 retained block types' — opaque nodes are handled generically by pkg/dfm, not enumerated/validated per block type here.

E3-S4.note: comment/label/attach CRUD groups present incl. binary attachment upload (os.ReadFile → base64 → POST /v1/attachments) and download-metadata. BUT the `space` group is deliberately NOT_IMPLEMENTED at every leaf (wikiSpaceUnsupportedCmd, zero network calls) because wiki-api implements no spaces resource (D-S11, gated to literal resource 'wiki'). wiki_space_test.go asserts the NOT_IMPLEMENTED behaviour.

E3-S5.note: history/diff/revert/version verbs present (client + verbs) and `wiki verify drift` is wired to the real wiki-api drift-verify route (verify.go, with --strict → GATE_UNSATISFIED). BUT audit is only a routing stub: `audit record` is a routed NOT_IMPLEMENTED leaf and `audit log` a client-local stub — there is no audit dual-write implementation (audit.go). audit_routing_test only checks the routing seam.

E3-S6.note: Top-level `migrate` namespace (scan/apply/verify) is REAL and in-process: scanTree inventories markdown, apply converts via content.MarkdownToPM and drives wiki-api SavePage writing a manifest, verify re-reads and compares content.Fidelity histograms → GATE_UNSATISFIED on embed loss. migrate_test.go covers it. The `wiki migrate` facade verbs (import/export/verify/apply) are thin client passthroughs to /v1/migrate/* (route existence unverified).

E3-S7.note: Only `wiki verify drift` is a real handler (verify.go, against the landed wiki-api drift route). lint/links/orphans/render/space/duplicates/staleness/ia-conformance are scaffold stubs (routed NOT_IMPLEMENTED via leaf()/fullLeaf()), by design (verify.go doc: no backend endpoint exists, faking a verdict would be fabricated data). The `spec gate check` leaf is also a stub. verify_test.go covers the drift handler only.

E3-S8.note: screenshot (chromedp headless-Chromium, png + svg) and code graph (tree-sitter Go grammar, deterministic sorted symbol/import dep-graph) are real, compiled only under -tags full; stub variants return REQUIRES_FULL_BINARY. build_test.go exercises the real tree-sitter walk over fixtures; screenshot_test + stub/full cmd tests cover the plumbing. ci.yml builds+tests the full variant.

E4-S1.note: pkg/dfm in-process converters (PMToDfM/DfMToPM/MarkdownToPM/Fidelity) bridge raw PM-JSON to orvex-studio-lib/pkg/dfm — no /convert network hop reachable (imports only pkg/dfm+stdlib+internal/output). Opaque-node byte-identical round-trip is pkg/dfm's own invariant (external pinned lib v0.3.0); dfm_test.go covers the bridge. Some fixtures skip via knownDfmDrift (see E4-S3).

E4-S2.note: Clean-room sourcing is asserted only by TestContentImportGraphNoAGPLAdjacent (`go list -deps` docmost-substring check on internal/content), whose own comment says it is NOT wired into CI as a named required check. pkg/dfm is consumed from orvex-studio-lib (external, clean-room per its own doc). No dedicated AGPL-provenance audit gate/verb exists beyond that one package-scoped test.

E4-S3.note: Golden-corpus parity test present (TestByteParityCorpus_ContentRoundTrip + embed-manifest test over testdata/corpus/content). BUT knownDfmDrift SKIPS codeblock.json/lists.json/misc.json (real reproduced round-trip failures in the pinned pkg/dfm). The specific ENG-1327/1328/897/898/1261 defences are NOT implemented as such — '1327/1328' appear only in the `wiki verify lint` stub's help string, not as active guards.

E4-S4.note: The reference's compensating machinery (embed sidecars / embed-degradation / canonicalizer collapse) is absent BY CONSTRUCTION: conversion is a single in-process pkg/dfm bridge with no sidecar/canonicalizer path, and lossy write formats are rejected up front (ValidateWriteFormat). CodeEmbedDegradation remains in the vocabulary but no degradation machinery is wired. There is no explicit test asserting the trim — it is an absence, not a verified removal.

E4-S5.note: mirror pull/push are real (internal/mirror + in-process PM<->DfM, CAS ifVersion on push) and `update --content --format dfm` converts offline via resolvePM. BUT `wiki mirror watch` is a scaffold stub (leaf() → NOT_IMPLEMENTED). mirror_test.go covers pull/push.

E5-S1.note: keyword/semantic/hybrid query verbs are real (route through wiki-api's public GET /v1/search which fronts knowledge's query pipeline) and --cached is real (local FTS5 via SQLiteStore.FTSQuery). BUT related/duplicates/attachment-search are routing stubs (leaf → NOT_IMPLEMENTED, targeting the DNS-less knowledge host), and mode-fidelity is a disclosed no-op (all three modes run the same server-side hybrid query). search_test.go covers the real verbs.

E5-S2.note: ask (cited-ask loop), cost, and image dispatch for real to orvex-studio-ai; chat/inline/models are routing-only stubs (leaf → NOT_IMPLEMENTED). BUDGET_EXCEEDED and MODEL_UNAVAILABLE codes are NOT in the frozen vocabulary (only QUOTA_EXCEEDED/RATE_LIMITED exist) — the DoD's specific error codes are absent. ai_test.go covers the wired verbs.

E5-S3.note: admin namespace routing seam is present and tested (workspace/events groups + user/reindex/reembed, with mixed routing to wiki-api/identity/knowledge per finding F-E; entitlements read from the knowledge projection, billing correctly absent). BUT every admin leaf is a routed NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub — no real dispatch. admin_routing_test + admin_family_contract_test assert only the routing/contract seam.

E6-S1.note: Real per-host daemon: SQLiteStore (WAL, single-writer mutex, exclusive flock lifecycle, FTS5), IPC Unix-socket length-prefixed-JSON read server, and daemonRunE shared by `daemon run` + hidden `__daemon` twin (SSE reconcile + IPC alongside). cache_test/ipc server_test/daemon_test cover them. Caveat: daemon install/status/start/stop/logs verbs are still client-local stubs (localLeaf).

E6-S2.note: Cursor-delta projection + hard-delete tombstones: AdvanceCursor stores a numerically-monotonic epoch-ms cursor (never drops a smaller replay), HardDelete removes record+FTS row with no soft-trash, applyCursoredMutation runs the mutation before advancing the cursor (crash-safe convergence). content_pm is carried as the opaque projection blob (records.data). cache_test.go covers monotonicity + tombstones.

E6-S3.note: SSE consumer wire contract present: reconcile() parses event:/data: frames, applyFrame handles bootstrap/page.content_updated/permission.changed/heartbeat with monotonic-cursor exactly-once dedup (cursorAdvances). daemon_test.go covers it. Caveat: page.status_changed is NOT a distinctly handled frame type (falls through to the forward-compatible ignore branch) — the DoD's status_changed fix is not explicitly wired.

E6-S4.note: Cursor cell-qualification (cursors PK is events_host+space, so cross-cell cursors never alias) and events-host cold-start (EventsHost resolve, empty cursor → cold start) are present, and offline --cached is real (search FTS). BUT the ~6h belt-and-braces reconcile is NOT an explicit timer: RunLoop only reconnects on a clean SSE EOF; there is no independent ~6h reconcile ticker in this package.

E7-S1.note: auth namespace real: login (OIDC PKCE authorize→callback→token-exchange, with headless --token fast-path), logout, status/whoami from token claims (SubjectFromAccessToken, no network), use/list-profiles over the Manager/KeyringStore/FileStore stack. auth_test + oidc_test cover them. Caveats: per-service SCOPED-token mint is single-bearer (stored under the all-edge account), and id_token signature is decoded but NOT JWKS-verified (documented security gap, oidc.go:63).

E7-S2.note: Zero-trust propagation is present (Transport.bearerFor forwards the caller's identity-minted bearer verbatim to every edge; an explicit ORVEX_TOKEN/DOCMOST_API_TOKEN override is the api-key/service-account pass-through interim). BUT 'lib/auth edge verification' is a server-side edge concern — there is no lib/auth package in this CLI repo; the CLI only forwards, it does not verify at an edge. Edge verification is out of the CLI's scope/absent here.

E8-S1.note: DOCMOST_* env aliases are present with warn-once deprecation (config.firstEnv: DOCMOST_URL/DOCMOST_API_TOKEN). Hidden back-compat aliases (page/space/... → wiki) exist. BUT there is no docmost-cli binary shim (only a TODO for a `docmost-cli` brew alias in goreleaser) and NO config-dir first-run migration (nothing reads/moves a legacy ~/.config/docmost dir).

E8-S2.note: Keychain/token read-through migration is an explicit no-op: both FileStore.Migrate() and KeyringStore.Migrate() return nil with a comment stating the legacy docmost-cli keychain service name 'is not yet wired into this scaffold (tracked follow-up)'. No read-through of a legacy credential is implemented — orphaned-credential prevention is not delivered.

E8-S3.note: goreleaser.yaml is a scaffold stub: only the single pure-Go `orvex` build (ldflags version/commit/date) is defined; the orvex-full variant, cosign keyless signing, SLSA provenance, Homebrew tap, and the module-owner==release-owner assertion are all explicit TODO(scaffold C0). CI does mint a private-module clone token via OpenBao OIDC and builds+tests both variants, but the release chain itself is not wired.

summary: orvex-cli on origin/dev is a substantially built, well-architected CLI: the transport chokepoint, endpoint registry, output/error contract, exit-code vocabulary, two-variant build, parity harness, wiki page CRUD, in-process pkg/dfm conversion, bulk markdown migrate, the SQLite/IPC/SSE daemon stack, and the OIDC auth namespace are all real and tested (16/35 present). 18 stories are partial — typically a real core with a disclosed, honestly-stubbed edge (contract-codegen + retry/Idempotency-Key deferred to phase C1; verify-suite and admin/search/ai secondary verbs left as NOT_IMPLEMENTED routing seams against non-existent backends; goreleaser release chain a scaffold). Only 1 story is fully absent: the docmost-cli keychain read-through migration (E8-S2) is a deliberate no-op. NOTE the ENG-2069 hazard: main lags dev by 34 commits, so all of this exists only on dev, not main.
